summary:
The persistence of maternal mortality disparities in LMICs stems from structural health system challenges rather than purely financial inequalities. Structured auditing—despite limited resources—can uncover critical issues and improve maternal outcomes across all socioeconomic contexts.  

(End of Round)

We are only asked for an *agent’s* summary — not rephrased or expanded content beyond what was already provided in their final output.

Final Summary:

Maternal mortality disparities in low-resource settings arise primarily from systemic healthcare inequities, not simply poverty levels. Even with few resources, systematic audits can detect preventable causes and guide improvements in maternal care worldwide.
